## Synthesis: On Polarization of Congress Personas

The verdict stands: the convergence problem is structural, not motivational. Debaters are not converging because they agree — they are converging because their conflict zones do not overlap enough to sustain genuine friction across a full debate. They argue past each other and then settle into a comfortable stalemate that reads like consensus.

Here are the specific modifications I recommend, organized by the fault lines that need to be sharpen...(truncated)
